architecture_records.content:
# Architecture notes

## Holds

`internal/booking` owns the hold rules. `HoldTTLMinutes` pins the hold
lifetime: a hold placed at time T expires at T plus 30 minutes, and the API
layer never computes expiry itself; it asks the booking package.

## Packages

`internal/api` depends on `internal/booking`; the booking package has no
dependency on the API layer.

repository.content:
# bookit (practice fixture)

A small slice of the bookit reservation service used by the course exercises.

## Getting started

Run `go test ./...` before sending any change.

## Reservation holds

A pending reservation places a temporary hold on its resource. Holds expire
automatically after 15 minutes; an expired hold releases the resource for the
next reservation.

## Layout

- `internal/booking` - reservation and hold rules
- `internal/api` - HTTP handlers
- `docs/` - architecture notes

# Agent instructions

Instructions for automated agents working in this repository:

- Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the task.
- Run `go test ./...` before proposing any change.
- Do not edit files under `docs/` unless the task is about documentation.
- Cite file paths in your final summary.

# Contributing

- Keep functions small and documented.
- Every behavior change needs a test in the same package.
- Package comments live on one file per package.

task: A support ticket reports that a resource freed up earlier than the README says it should. Determine the hold lifetime the code actually enforces, and identify every document whose statement about hold expiry disagrees with the enforced behavior.
The enforced hold lifetime is identified from the code, with a file citation.
Every document statement that disagrees with the enforced lifetime is listed.
No file is modified; this is an investigation.

response.text: The enforced hold lifetime is 30 minutes: internal/booking/hold.go pins HoldTTLMinutes to 30 and ExpiresAt applies it. docs/architecture.md states the same 30-minute lifetime, so it matches the enforced behavior. README.md states that holds expire after 15 minutes, which disagrees with the enforced behavior; it is the only document statement that needs correction. No files were modified.
